Investment Drivers

D5 zoning, room-renting income, and location demand create multiple paths for operation and exit strategy.

D5 Zoning Flexibility

City-confirmed D5 zoning supports SFR, duplex, triplex, or quad options, widening the buyer pool and exit paths.

Renovated Condition

The properties are recently renovated, already partially occupied, and positioned for continued room-rent placement.

Room-Renting Strategy

Room rents are projected at $500-$650 per room per month, with utilities tenant-paid.

Utility NOI Upside

NOI may be significantly increased by sub-metering the properties and charging for utilities and/or placing smart thermostats.

Subsidized Housing Fit

Section 8, group housing, and professional rental demand are listed as strong use cases.

Location Demand

Haughville, West Indy, Crown Hill, and North Indy locations benefit from downtown, IUPUI, Butler, transit, and amenity proximity.
